deep_dive.executive_summary: At $32.84, the base case is 0.7% per year over seven years on normalized starting owner cash of $0.80 per share. Hims & Hers is a scaled DTC telehealth platform with FY2025 revenue $2.35 billion, operating income $105.6 million, and operating cash flow $300 million, but Q1 2026 revenue growth slowed to ~4% year over year. The company carries $974 million of convertible debt and ~$700 million of investment securities alongside $222 million of cash. Compounded GLP-1 semaglutide is an embedded product line under active FDA scrutiny. Moat is eroding (regulatory and competitive pressure on weigh...

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: Revenue moved +59.0% versus the prior period (1,476,514.0 to 2,347,637.0).
Operating income moved +70.6% versus the prior period (61,903.0 to 105,613.0).
FDA statement on restricting mass-marketed compounded GLP-1 semaglutide named Hims & Hers directly; follows prior FDA warning letters on compounded semaglutide
FY2025 revenue $2.35B (+59% YoY); operating income $105.6M; OCF $300M; net income $128.4M
Issued ~$970M convertible senior notes; convertible debt $972.6M on balance sheet at year-end
Expanded owned pharmacy infrastructure: 503B outsourcing facility, peptide manufacturing, and lab testing facilities integrated with telehealth platform
Q1 2025 revenue $586M (+111% YoY versus Q1 2024); GLP-1 weight-loss category scaling
FY2024 revenue $1.48B (+69% YoY); operating income $61.9M; first full year of major weight-loss contribution
International expansion: platform live in Canada, UK, and EU (Germany, Ireland, France, Spain)
Acquisitions added goodwill and intangibles; finite-lived intangibles net $196M by FY2025
Public company via SPAC merger (Oaktree Acquisition Corp.); NYSE-listed Hims & Hers Health
Company founded; consumer-first telehealth platform for stigmatized health categories (Hims men's health, Hers women's health)

industry.structure: U.S. direct-to-consumer telehealth is fragmented among Hims & Hers, Ro, LifeMD, and traditional pharmacy/retail clinics. Vertical integration (owned pharmacies, 503B compounding) raises entry barriers but invites FDA and state-board scrutiny on compounded GLP-1 alternatives to branded obesity drugs.
industry.share_shift: Hims & Hers gained share in online weight loss and men's/women's wellness as GLP-1 demand surged (FY2025 revenue $2.35B, +59% YoY). Q1 2026 revenue growth slowed to ~4% YoY, suggesting normalization or regulatory headwinds on compounded semaglutide.
industry.trend: Telehealth convenience, subscription economics, and influencer marketing remain tailwinds; February 2026 FDA focus on compounded GLP-1 mass marketing is the dominant near-term regulatory swing factor. Peers include Ro, Teladoc, Amazon Clinic, and Novo/Lilly branded channels.

Revenue growth moved from +49.2% to +3.8% YoY in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
*Primary risk: FDA and state regulators restrict compounded GLP-1 semaglutide marketing and fulfillment, removing a major recent growth driver and compressing gross margin.
